goal: Verify whether the Gmail SMTP app password has actually expired and quantify the blast radius — how many of the 139 provider_accepted sends are at risk of follow-up failure.
success_criteria: SMTP credential status confirmed (working vs expired) with test send
Operator alerted if credentials are expired — this is ORANGE (credential change needed, but not credential_change itself — alert only)

slices.task: Step 1: Test SMTP right now: GMAIL_ADDRESS='owner@outboundautonomy.com' python3 /Users/ryleebenson/Desktop/OPENCLAW/scripts/gmail-smtp-send.py --to owner@outboundautonomy.com --subject 'SMTP credential test' --body 'Testing Gmail app password validity — 2026-04-29 14:04 MDT'. Capture the full output including any 535 error. Step 2: Check the last 5 successful sends for timing: sqlite3 /Users/ryleebenson/Desktop/OPENCLAW/data/crm.sqlite 'SELECT id,lead_id,recipient,status,created_at FROM email_attempts WHERE status="provider_accepted" ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 5'. Step 3: Write findings to artifacts/missions/smtp-credential-alert-20260429-1404/forge-smtp-test.md with: test result (working/expired), last successful send timestamp, and blast radius assessment. If SMTP is expired, this is ORANGE — the heartbeat action #331 already documented the 535 error, but we need independent verification before alerting.

reasoning: CRM action #331 documents 'SMTP password expired (535 error)' — if true, the entire 139-send pipeline's follow-up capability is at risk. This is the single most important diagnostic right now. YELLOW for the test (read-only API call), but the finding may escalate to ORANGE (credential issue requiring operator attention). Single engineering slice — independent test of the credential before raising an alarm.
